reasoning:
The base rate of ACLED 'Riots' in Libya over the past 360 days is approximately 4.0 per month. Assuming an overdispersed (e.g. negative binomial) distribution, a randomly selected 30-day period has a ~35-40% chance of exceeding its annual mean (which effectively requires >=5 riots). 

In mid-May 2025, Libya entered a major political crisis following intense militia clashes on May 12-13 and the death of a prominent militia leader. This triggered widespread anti-government protests demanding the resignation of the Prime Minister. On May 16, a group embedded in the protests attempted to storm the PM's office, resulting in the death of a security member. Protests have raged across multiple cities at least through late May. Given this severe spike in political unrest and typical ACLED coding (where storming buildings or mob clashes are recorded as Riots), the 30-day window for June 15 (May 16 - June 15) is highly likely to feature significantly more than 4 riots. 

Moving forward, the crisis could persist into the July 8 window (June 8 - July 8), keeping the probability elevated. By September and December, the situation is more likely to have stabilized or changed form (e.g. escalating into full militia Battles or returning to baseline), so the estimates for these distant dates revert closer to the structural base rate of ~40%.
